# Combobox

A searchable listbox for finding and selecting from a long set of options.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Anatomy](#anatomy)
- [Controlled behavior](#controlled-behavior)
- [Filtering and states](#filtering-and-states)
- [Accessibility](#accessibility)
- [Choosing a picker](#choosing-a-picker)

## Installation

Add the Combobox component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/combobox
```

## Usage

Use Combobox when users know roughly what they want but scanning the complete option list would be slow. Compose its trigger, search input, filtered viewport, groups, and items; bind `v-model` to the selected value.

**Searchable selection example**

```vue
<template>
  <Combobox v-model="value">
    <ComboboxAnchor class="w-64">
      <ComboboxTrigger as-child>
        <Button
          variant="outline"
          role="combobox"
          class="w-full justify-between"
        >
          {{ selectedLabel }}
          <ChevronDownIcon class="size-4 opacity-50" />
        </Button>
      </ComboboxTrigger>
    </ComboboxAnchor>

    <ComboboxList class="w-64">
      <ComboboxInput placeholder="Search frameworks…" />
      <ComboboxEmpty>No framework found.</ComboboxEmpty>
      <ComboboxViewport>
        <ComboboxGroup heading="Frameworks">
          <ComboboxItem
            v-for="framework in frameworks"
            :key="framework.value"
            :value="framework.value"
          >
            {{ framework.label }}
            <ComboboxItemIndicator>
              <CheckIcon class="size-4" />
            </ComboboxItemIndicator>
          </ComboboxItem>
        </ComboboxGroup>
      </ComboboxViewport>
    </ComboboxList>
  </Combobox>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  Combobox,
  ComboboxAnchor,
  ComboboxEmpty,
  ComboboxGroup,
  ComboboxInput,
  ComboboxItem,
  ComboboxItemIndicator,
  ComboboxList,
  ComboboxTrigger,
  ComboboxViewport,
} from '@/Components/Combobox'
import { CheckIcon, ChevronDownIcon } from '@lucide/vue'
import { computed, ref } from 'vue'

const frameworks = [
  { value: 'next.js', label: 'Next.js' },
  { value: 'sveltekit', label: 'SvelteKit' },
  { value: 'nuxt', label: 'Nuxt' },
  { value: 'remix', label: 'Remix' },
  { value: 'astro', label: 'Astro' },
]

const value = ref('')
const selectedLabel = computed(() =>
  frameworks.find(framework => framework.value === value.value)?.label
  ?? 'Select a framework',
)
</script>
```

## Anatomy

`Combobox` owns selection and open state. `ComboboxAnchor` positions the list, `ComboboxTrigger` opens it, and `ComboboxList` portals the popup. Inside the list, place `ComboboxInput` before `ComboboxViewport`, then render groups and items. Use `ComboboxEmpty` to explain an empty result instead of leaving a blank popup.

The trigger in the example uses `as-child` so Reka behavior is merged onto the Button without an extra wrapper. Preserve a single interactive child and read the [composition fundamentals](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/fundamentals/composition) before wrapping trigger behavior in another component.

## Controlled behavior

Use `v-model` when the selected value affects other UI. Use `default-value` only for an initial uncontrolled selection. Bind `v-model:open` only when application logic must open or close the list; otherwise let the component manage the popup.

Set `multiple` with an array model for a true multi-select. For object values, pass `by` so items compare by a stable key. Keep primitive ids in form state when that is sufficient—it makes serialization and equality easier to reason about.

## Filtering and states

The root filters registered items from the search term by default. Use `ignore-filter` only when the displayed results are already filtered by application logic. Disable unavailable items individually and disable the root only when no selection can be made. Keep the empty message distinct from a loading message.

## Accessibility

Give the trigger an accessible name and show the selected label, not only an icon. The composed primitives provide listbox keyboard navigation, highlighting, and selection behavior; avoid replacing items with arbitrary clickable elements. Use a visible field label and description when the choice belongs to a form.

## Choosing a picker

> **Use Select for a short custom list: [Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/select)**
>
> If the list is short enough to scan without search, Select is simpler and keeps the same custom popup interaction.

> **Use Native Select for browser-native behavior: [Native Select](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/native-select)**
>
> Prefer Native Select for straightforward choices where platform-native mobile pickers, autofill, and ordinary form behavior matter most.

> **Use Command for actions: [Command](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/command)**
>
> If filtering launches actions or navigation rather than selecting a form value, compose Command with the appropriate overlay instead.

Hosted documentation: [Combobox](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/combobox)
